definition: Markets are simultaneously pricing mutually inconsistent energy transition scenarios: clean energy equities at 25-35x P/E imply rapid deployment; E&P CDS at 150-250bp imply no stranded asset risk; copper at $8-11k/t implies neither demand acceleration nor supply scarcity. These three signals cannot all be correct.
evidence: Clean energy equities at 25-35x forward P/E
Equity deployment rates demand more copper than current prices incentivize
open_questions: Is the inconsistency arbitrageable or does it reflect genuine uncertainty about pathway?
How long can mutually inconsistent pricing persist?
